subject: FW: From Howard Gardner
body:
From: hgasst [mailto:hgasst@pz.harvard.edu] 
Sent: Monday, June 09, 2008 11:13 AM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: From Howard Gardner

 

June 9, 2008

 

To Jeffrey Epstein

>From Howard Gardner

 

Hi, Jeffrey. I am glad that we had a chance to talk last Friday.
Spirited and informative as always.

 

You asked about ideas with reference to education going forward. With
the snail mail version of this note, I am enclosing Dave Perkins'
informal essay on 'the relevance gap'-or why trigonometry is less
important than probability, and why knowing how to learn and wanting to
learn are king.  I also enclose the little essay I wrote on the three
systems of education in the US and how they are 'wanting'  different
things. You might also refer your friends on the Virgin Island to my
Five Minds for the Future and, as a contrast case, Daniel Pink's A Whole
new Mind.

 

Be well and I hope to see you in the fall.

 

With best wishes.

 

Howard Gardner

Hobbs Professor of Cognition and Education 

Harvard Graduate School of Education

14 Appian Way

Larsen Hall 201

Cambridge, MA 02138

 

Voice: 617-496-4929

Fax: 617-496-4855

www.howardgardner.com

e-mail: howard@pz.harvard.edu

 

NOTE REGARDING SPAM FILTER: If you reply to this communication, please
be sure to include the name "Howard Gardner" in the text of your
response. Otherwise, the spam filter may delete your message.
body_html:
From: hgasst [mailto:hgasst@pz.harvard.edu]
Sent: Monday, June 09, 2008 11:13 AM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: From Howard Gardner

 

June 9, 2008

 

To Jeffrey Epstein

From Howard Gardner

 

Hi, Jeffrey. I am glad that we had a chance to talk last Friday. Spirited and informative as always.

 

You asked about ideas with reference to education going forward. With the snail mail version of this note, I am enclosing Dave Perkins’ informal essay on ‘the relevance gap’—or why trigonometry is less important than probability, and why knowing how to learn and wanting to learn are king.  I also enclose the little essay I wrote on the three systems of education in the US and how they are ‘wanting’  different things. You might also refer your friends on the Virgin Island to my Five Minds for the Future and, as a contrast case, Daniel Pink’s A Whole new Mind.

 

Be well and I hope to see you in the fall.

 

With best wishes.

 

Howard Gardner

Hobbs Professor of Cognition and Education

Harvard Graduate School of Education

14 Appian Way

Larsen Hall 201

Cambridge, MA 02138

 

Voice: 617-496-4929

Fax: 617-496-4855

www.howardgardner.com

e-mail: howard@pz.harvard.edu

 

NOTE REGARDING SPAM FILTER: If you reply to this communication, please be sure to include the name "Howard Gardner" in the text of your response. Otherwise, the spam filter may delete your message.
